subject: English
skill: Main Idea, Key Details, and Text Features

lesson.story_mission: Join the Info Investigators to find main ideas, details, text features, and idea connections.
lesson.objective: I can determine the main idea, choose supporting details, use text features, and explain how ideas connect.
lesson.concept_explanation: Informational readers first name the topic, then state the main idea as a complete sentence. Key details must support that idea, and text features such as headings, captions, diagrams, and maps help readers locate or understand information.
lesson.common_misconception: Readers may confuse a one-word topic with a complete main idea or choose details that are not supported.
lesson.real_world_connection: Main idea and text features help readers learn from science articles, directions, websites, and textbooks.
lesson.lesson_summary: Find the topic, state the main idea, match key details, use text features, and connect related ideas.
lesson.mini_lesson: Look at the title and first sentence for the topic. Combine important details to state the main idea. Then use headings, captions, diagrams, and maps to understand how ideas fit together.

audio.text: topic, main idea, key detail, text feature, caption, heading, diagram
audio.label: Listen

worked_examples.text: Watch: The weather station passage is about a class weather station. The main idea is that it helps students collect and compare weather data.
worked_examples.correct_answer: A weather station helps students collect and compare weather data.
worked_examples.passage: A class weather station helps students collect daily weather data. A thermometer shows temperature, a rain gauge measures rainfall, and a wind sock shows wind direction. Students compare the data to notice weather patterns each week.
worked_examples.main_idea: A weather station helps students collect and compare weather data.

audio.text: A class weather station helps students collect daily weather data. A thermometer shows temperature, a rain gauge measures rainfall, and a wind sock shows wind direction. Students compare the data to notice weather patterns each week.
audio.label: Listen

worked_examples.text: Demo: A diagram would show the parts of the weather station. The feature is useful because it helps readers see what each tool does.
worked_examples.feature_purpose: shows the parts of a weather station
worked_examples.title: Building a Class Weather Station

audio.text: A diagram shows the parts of a weather station.
audio.label: Listen

hint_ladder: Choose details that support the whole passage.
Use text features to learn where information is and what it means.

misconception_bank.topic_main_idea_confusion: A reader names a topic word instead of a complete main idea.
misconception_bank.detail_selection_error: A reader picks an interesting fact that does not support the main idea.
misconception_bank.text_feature_confusion: A reader names a feature but misses its purpose.
misconception_bank.unsupported_detail: A reader chooses a detail that is not stated or supported by the text.

audio.text: Bees help many plants grow by moving pollen from flower to flower. Farmers depend on bees for fruits such as apples and berries. People can help bees by planting flowers and avoiding sprays that hurt insects.
audio.label: Listen

questions.feature_purpose: explains what bees are doing in a photo
questions.title: Why Bees Matter

story.text: Read this page to enter the Main Idea, Key Details, and Text Features mission. Think about how careful readers notice the author’s clues before they answer. Press Start Mission when you are ready to read, listen, and build the skill step by step.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: Read this page to learn the strategy for topic, main idea, key details, text features, and idea relationships. Look at the model words and connect each vocabulary word to the reading job. Press Next to continue after you can explain the strategy in your own words.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Read this page while the worked example shows the prompt and model. Step one, read the text carefully. Step two, find the clue that answers the question. Step three, choose the answer and check why the final answer is supported. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: Read this page to try the demo with support. Use the visual model to organize the reading clues before answering. Press Next to continue when you can tell why the answer fits the text.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Read this page before practice. Listen to the passage when the Listen button helps, then press Read Question so the question is clear. Choose your answer when you are ready, and use the hint if you need help.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: Read this page before the challenge. This question asks you to use the same strategy with less help. Press Read Question, reread the important part of the text, and answer carefully, then continue.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: Read this page for the checkpoint. Show what you know by reading the text, pressing Read Question, and checking your evidence before you answer. Answer carefully, then continue to see your results.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: Read this page to celebrate your progress. The badge means you practiced reading closely and explaining your thinking. Press Next to see your profile and think about where you can use this skill next.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: Read this page to review your learner profile. Look at the skill, the badge, and the next practice option. Continue to Skill Practice or go back to the hub when you are ready.
profile.label: Read This Page
